title: Erdős #399 companion (Cambie): no n! = x^4 + y^4 with gcd(x,y)=1, xy>1
informal_statement: A known companion result to Erdos Problem #399, attributed to Cambie: there is no solution to n! = x^4 + y^4 with gcd(x,y) = 1 and x*y > 1. This does NOT resolve Erdos #399 itself (which asks about n! = x^k +/- y^k for xy>1, k>2 in general; the headline question already has a known non-solution-free answer via Barfield's 10! = 48^4 - 36^4, which is a different, unrelated construction from this companion).
informal_proof_idea: Mod-8 argument. Fourth powers mod 8 detect parity (a^4 % 8 = a % 2). If gcd(x,y)=1 then x,y are not both even, so x^4+y^4 is 1 or 2 mod 8, never 0. For n>=4, 8 | n!, contradicting the mod-8 residue. For n<=3, n! <= 6 while xy>1 forces x^4+y^4 >= 17. Either way n! != x^4+y^4.
formal_statement_pp: theorem erdos_399_cambie (n x y : ℕ) (hxy : Nat.Coprime x y) (h1 : 1 < x * y) : Nat.factorial n ≠ x ^ 4 + y ^ 4

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; statement cross-checked against the sibling repo's already-kernel_verified proof of the same result

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only a known COMPANION result to Erdos #399, not Erdos #399 itself. Erdos #399 (n! = x^k +/- y^k, xy>1, k>2) is already known to be NOT solution-free in general via Barfield's construction 10! = 48^4 - 36^4 (unrelated to this proof). This packet's theorem is the narrower Cambie companion (no n! = x^4+y^4 with gcd(x,y)=1, xy>1) and makes no claim about the general problem. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode d2aaae74, problem_version 8ee2e559-41dd-4bd6-ad6b-df16125a0966), submitted as a SubmitModule (helper theorem pow4_mod8 + root theorem erdos_399_cambie), kernel_verified on the first submission after one transport-format retry (root_theorem.proof_format=raw_lean_block was required to preserve the bullet/case-split structure; the first attempt used the postfix '!' factorial notation and hit an unrelated parser ambiguity between '!' and '≠' in the SubmitModule file-assembly context -- switched to the unambiguous Nat.factorial spelling, which resolved it cleanly). Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain (Lean v4.32.0-rc1, mathlib 360da6fa) rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo (F:\Github\mnehmos.llm-driven-proof-search.environment\ErdosProblems\erdos-399\proof\Erdos399_cambie.lean) already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy (docs/proofsearch-integration.md), a proof checked some other way is a candidate, not evidence, until this pinned kernel checks it inside a tracked episode. First packet in the frontier/erdos lane (previously 0 packets, Phase 5 not started). training.eligibility is quarantined by default (not public_train) per the roadmap's conservative-import/default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets -- this is a companion result, not a solved open problem, but it has not gone through a formal external review (fidelity_status remains 'attested', not 'verified'), so it stays quarantined until that review happens.
